hard.account_name: Shenzhen WIK Domestic Appliances CO Ltd.
hard.owner_title: Vice President Asia Pacific Japan & ME

north_star_verdict.headline: WIK's flagship agentic-AI pilot (ANA + Intake) is alive with Rouven Ehniss driving it, but momentum thinned after the 14 Jun Horizon decline and a 2 Jul 'review still in progress' reply; no board budget owner is engaged, HK/China AI-compliance is unresolved since 6 Mar, and scope was cut to a $150k quick-win pilot.
north_star_verdict.math: 44 days to close (close 2026-08-19, today 2026-07-06). Created 2026-03-08, 120 days in cycle, Formal Evaluation the whole time with no submission-date milestone stamped. Last SF activity 2026-07-02: buyer emailed that their internal review is still running and next steps come 'no later than the end of July' (a buyer reply, but a deferral, not an advance). Prior cadence: proposal/commercials 22 May, amount cut 250k to 150k 6 Jun, Horizon declined 14 Jun, review-in-progress 2 Jul. Required pace to hold 19 Aug: board budget approval + paid-pilot scoping + contracting in ~6 weeks while WIK's own review runs to end-July, which is not credible.
evidence: Buyer engagement is thinning: WIK declined the 21-22 Jul Horizon invite (email 14 Jun) and on 2 Jul said their internal review is still in progress with next steps only by end-July, so Zycus is chasing rather than being pulled.

No economic buyer is engaged 120 days in. Budget approval sits with WIK management/board (Next Step 6 Jun 'Going to mgmt for budget approval'); a CPO surfaced only as a Horizon invitee (22 Jun) and Zycus has never met them.

HK/China AI-usage restriction raised by WIK's Head of IT on the 6 Mar call ('would it be any concern to us being here out in China, Hong Kong') is still open after four months and gates any China/HK rollout and the supplier base.

Scope and budget were cut from $250k to a $150k quick-win pilot on 6 Jun; Rouven wants a small proof to win board buy-in ('we don't want to go now into the full transformation... we need these quick wins to have buy-in of our board'), signalling budget caution and status-quo risk.
Deal remains single-threaded on Rouven Ehniss (Evaluator); Legal (Josslyn Deng), budget control (Karen Zheng) and IT are contact roles but unengaged.

metrics.narrative: WIK's business problem is clear but never quantified. On the 6 Mar call Rouven Ehniss (GM Global Sourcing) framed the goal as AI 'quick wins' the team can take to the board: 'we are looking for some big wins which we can present also as part of our digitalization strategy to say this is possible. This is how much it will cost us.' Next Step 6 Mar records 'Interest in AI quick wins around negotiations' and 27 Mar 'ANA sounds a great use case.' No cost-of-delay, negotiation-cycle baseline, savings target or headcount figure has been articulated across any call, email or field. The pain owner is Rouven; the move is to co-build a one-page ROI on WIK's two named processes so he can defend the budget to the board.

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits with WIK's management/board, not with anyone Zycus has engaged. Next Step 6 Jun states 'Going to mgmt for budget approval' and Rouven said on 6 Mar he needs 'the buy-in of our board and of our organization.' A CPO appears as a Horizon invitee (Next Step 22 Jun 'CPO coming to Horizon') but WIK declined and Zycus has never met them. The nearest Salesforce-named budget-control contact is Karen Zheng, Head of Purchasing Controlling (she also attended the 6 Mar call as the purchasing-control lead), but she has not engaged since. The economic buyer is inferred as the board/CPO layer that Rouven is escalating to; the move is to have Rouven name and introduce that budget owner before the end-July review closes.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Karen Zheng (Head of Purchasing Controlling)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are documented from the call and the process work. WIK selected two live processes to test Zycus against, shared 21 Mar: 'Supplier nomination process for indirect materials' and 'Annual price negotiation process for production materials.' Next Step 27 Apr confirms 'Decision to move forward based on requirements matching.' Beyond functional fit, the buyer's stated criteria are: AI/agentic capability tied to their digitalisation strategy (the primary lever), HK/China AI-usage compliance (Head of IT, 6 Mar), integration with SAP S/4HANA and their in-house OA/eProcurement platform via iSaaS, and SSO. Rouven was explicit that functional coverage is table stakes; Zycus wins or loses on linking 'AI capabilities versus your AI strategy' to generate quick wins.
sources: Avoma 6 Mar call (Head of IT; Rouven Ehniss)

decision_process.narrative: Reading the sequence: NDA (signed 18 Mar via DocuSign) then two use cases shared (21 Mar), Zycus presales process-mapping delivered (5 May), commercials/pricing walkthrough (18-22 May), then escalation to management for budget approval (6 Jun). The buyer is now in an internal review whose outcome Rouven committed to deliver 'no later than the end of July' (2 Jul email). The approval chain is board/management budget sign-off gated by a demonstrated quick-win business case; Rouven owns the internal socialisation. The process is genuine but seller-paced right now, with the decisive gate (board budget) sitting inside WIK where Zycus has no direct line.

paper_process.narrative: Early contracting mechanics are visible. The NDA was executed 18 Mar through a WIK-created DocuSign envelope, with Carl Kimball as the sole Zycus signer and the contracting entity Zycus Infotech Singapore Pte Ltd. WIK's Head of Legal, Josslyn Deng, is a mapped contact but has not engaged on any commercial paper. As a paid ANA + Intake pilot this is likely to contract as an Order Form plus a pilot SOW rather than a full MSA, but nothing is drafted. Visibility on the real paper process will come only once the board approves budget; the move is to line up Josslyn Deng and confirm the pilot contracting vehicle in parallel with the budget ask.

identify_pain.narrative: WIK's pain is inefficient, fragmented indirect procurement and manual requisition/negotiation workflows they want to modernise with AI. On the 6 Mar call the Head of IT described end users raising purchase requests and approvals on a customised in-house office-automation platform plus a separate eProcurement tool, and asked what Zycus could replace. Rouven anchored the pain to negotiations and intake, seeking 'quick wins' to prove value before a full transformation, positioned inside WIK's post-S/4HANA-migration digitalisation strategy (Next Step 28 Apr: 'this Agentic AI use case will be the 1st one'). The pain owner is Rouven Ehniss; it is confirmed and strategic, but its financial magnitude is not yet quantified.
sources: Avoma 6 Mar call (Head of IT; Rouven Ehniss)

champion.narrative: Rouven Ehniss (GM Global Sourcing, Evaluator, PRIMARY contact) is a developing champion. What has developed: he convened the 6 Mar discovery with his full team, drove the NDA, selected and shared the two test processes, took the presales process-mapping, engaged on commercials, and is now escalating to the board for budget ('Going to mgmt for budget approval', 6 Jun). What is still missing: he has not given Zycus access to the economic buyer/board, the deal is single-threaded on him with Legal, Finance and IT unengaged, and his engagement cooled in June-July (declined Horizon 14 Jun, internal review still open 2 Jul, Zycus initiating the follow-ups). He is a real advocate but not yet a strong, multi-threaded champion who can pull the deal through the board.

competition.narrative: No external vendor is named in any source across 120 days: Competitors__c is blank and no rival appears in the 6 Mar call, emails, tasks or next steps. WIK is positioning Zycus as its FIRST agentic-AI use case within a broader digitalisation initiative (Next Step 28 Apr), which reads as sole-source or first-mover rather than a bake-off. The real contest is therefore internal: the status quo / do-nothing path (Rouven wants a small quick-win pilot to earn board buy-in before committing) and WIK's existing in-house OA and eProcurement tooling. The wedge is to make the agentic quick win concrete and board-fundable so inertia loses; the risk is that a cautious board defers rather than a competitor beating us.

deal_movement.summary: Steady early-stage build from Mar-May (NDA, use cases, process mapping, commercials) that has slowed into a June-July holding pattern: scope cut to a $150k pilot, Horizon declined, and the buyer's board review deferring the decision to end-July.

items.change: NDA executed via DocuSign (Carl Kimball sole Zycus signer)
WIK shared its two evaluation processes (indirect supplier nomination; annual price negotiation)
Commercials and pricing methodology walked through; proposal for ANA + Intake issued
Opportunity owner changed from Guillaume Pasquet to Carl Kimball
Amount reduced from $250k to $150k (quick-win pilot framing)
WIK declined the 21-22 Jul Horizon invitation
Buyer replied internal review still in progress; next steps by end of July

competitive_position.summary: No external competitor has ever been named (Competitors__c blank; nothing in the 6 Mar call, emails, tasks or next steps), so this reads as a first-mover / sole-source agentic-AI selection. The single strongest CURRENT threat is WIK's own status quo: a cautious board wanting a small, cheap quick-win pilot (scope cut to $150k on 6 Jun) could defer rather than commit, and WIK's existing in-house OA and eProcurement tooling is the incumbent alternative. The play is to make the agentic quick win board-fundable so inertia, not a rival, is what we beat.

competitors.name: Status quo / do-nothing (phased internal digitalisation)
competitors.quote: we don't want to go now into the full transformation, but we are looking for some big wins... we need these quick wins to have buy-in of our board
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver a board-ready ROI and a low-risk paid pilot on ANA + Intake so approving beats deferring.

competitors.name: In-house OA / eProcurement platform (incumbent tooling)
competitors.quote: our purchasing requisition forms, all of the flows and approvals are all done on the (OA)... the eProcurement side we may review between Zycus and that to see what Zycus can replace
competitors.how_we_win: Show iSaaS integration to their SAP S/4HANA and OA platform, then Intake as the single front door replacing manual PR/PO flows.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong alignment on the AI/agentic vision and integration story; the exposure is a quantified value case and unresolved HK/China AI compliance.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers dominate: arm the champion with a board-ready ROI, multi-thread past Rouven, and clear the one technical blocker.
flags: Turn the flagship-AI narrative into a one-page ROI on WIK's two named processes so Rouven can win board budget before the end-July review.
Break single-threading: engage Karen Zheng (budget control), Josslyn Deng (Legal) and the Head of IT, and get Rouven to name the board/CPO budget owner.
Close the HK/China AI-usage question in writing to remove the only technical blocker to a China/HK pilot and reassure the supplier-base concern.

champion_strength.summary: Rouven Ehniss is a developing champion who drove NDA, use-case selection, process mapping, commercials and the budget escalation, but he lacks proven board/EB access, the deal is single-threaded on him, and his engagement cooled in June-July (Horizon decline, deferred review).
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator, Primary); Avoma 6 Mar call; Next_Step__c 6 Jun 2026; Task 14 Jun and 2 Jul 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Merlin agentic and Intake narrative landed strongly on the 6 Mar call and the buyer explicitly frames Zycus as their first agentic-AI use case; positioning is a strength, not a gap.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 9.0 / Strong is corroborated by the buyer's own words: WIK is treating agentic AI as the primary selection criterion ('the 1st one: Intake, ANA, Strategic Sourcing') and proactively managing HK/China AI restrictions. Not over-rated.

items.detail: No economic buyer engaged 120 days in; board/CPO budget owner unnamed and unmet.
Scope cut from $250k to a $150k quick-win pilot; board wants a cheap proof, raising do-nothing/deferral risk.
items.source: Field history 6 Jun 2026; Avoma 6 Mar call

items.detail: HK/China AI-usage compliance for users and supplier base unresolved since 6 Mar; gates a China/HK rollout.
Close date 19 Aug not credible: buyer's internal review runs to end-July, leaving no room for approval, pilot scoping and contracting.
Single-threaded on Rouven Ehniss; Legal, Finance and IT contacts unengaged.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement cooled through June-July: Horizon declined, review deferred to end-July, and Zycus initiating the follow-ups rather than being pulled.
items: Declined the 21-22 Jul Horizon invite (14 Jun)
Internal review 'still in progress', next steps only by end-July (2 Jul)
Rep-initiated follow-ups on 14 Jun and 2 Jul, not buyer-initiated

scope_change.from: $250k broader agentic scope (Intake, ANA, Strategic Sourcing)
scope_change.detail: Amount cut from $250k to $150k on 6 Jun; Rouven explicitly wants a small quick-win pilot to earn board buy-in before any full transformation (6 Mar call), a defensive, phased posture.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is the front-runner for WIK's flagship agentic-AI pilot (ANA + Intake) with GM Global Sourcing Rouven Ehniss driving it, but the deal is stalled on WIK's internal budget review, an unmapped board-level economic buyer, and unresolved HK/China AI-compliance, with scope already cut from $250k to a $150k quick-win pilot.

win_position.text: We sit as WIK's chosen partner for their first agentic-AI use case (ANA + Intake) with a Strong AI-fit signal and GM Global Sourcing Rouven Ehniss championing it, and no rival has ever been named (Next Step 28 Apr; 6 Mar call).

No economic buyer is engaged 120 days in: budget sits with WIK's board/CPO, whom Zycus has never met, and Rouven cannot yet get us in front of them (Next Step 6 Jun 'Going to mgmt for budget approval').
Scope was cut from $250k to a $150k quick-win pilot on 6 Jun and the buyer wants a cheap board proof, signalling budget caution and a real do-nothing/deferral risk rather than a loss to a competitor.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum thinned: WIK declined the 21-22 Jul Horizon invite (14 Jun) and on 2 Jul said their internal review is still running with next steps only by end-July, so Zycus is chasing.
Commercials are live: Zycus walked WIK through pricing methodology and issued the ANA + Intake proposal (18-22 May), and Rouven requested a follow-up meeting (18 May).

customer_commitment.text: WIK signed the NDA (18 Mar), shared two live processes for analysis (21 Mar), and frames Zycus as their flagship agentic-AI rollout tied to a board-level digitalisation strategy (Next Step 28 Apr).
Commitment is soft: the buyer wants a small quick-win pilot to earn board buy-in, has not approved budget, and set no firm decision date beyond 'realise something in 2026' (Rouven, 6 Mar).

deal_risk.text: HK/China AI-usage restriction raised by WIK's Head of IT on the 6 Mar call is unresolved four months on and could block deployment for China/HK users and the supplier base.
Single-threaded on Rouven Ehniss: Legal (Josslyn Deng), budget control (Karen Zheng) and IT are contact roles but unengaged, so a champion wobble stalls the deal.
The 19 Aug close date is not credible: board approval, pilot scoping and contracting cannot complete in 44 days while WIK's review runs to end-July.

deal_momentum.evidence: Horizon declined 14 Jun; review deferred to end-July 2 Jul; Zycus-initiated follow-ups.

customer_commitment.evidence: Wants a small quick-win pilot for board buy-in; scope cut to $150k; no firm decision date.

deal_risk.evidence: Unmapped board EB, unresolved HK/China AI compliance, single-threaded, close date not credible.

customer_preference.evidence: Zycus-specific engagement and appetite (NDA, use cases shared, proposal requested, 'ANA sounds a great use case'), but no explicit competitive comparison and the decision is deferred to an internal board review.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain and strategic intent articulated (AI quick wins in negotiation, digitalisation strategy) but no quantified ROI, savings or cycle-time numbers.

items.expected_effect: Opens access to the economic buyer before the end-July review and de-risks the budget gate.

items.expected_effect: Removes the one technical blocker to a China/HK pilot and reassures the supplier-base concern.

items.expected_effect: Turns the quick-win intent into a bounded, contractable pilot that can close after budget approval.
